data.title: Update: Buyback program: Week 14 2026 repurchase transactions disclosed.
data.description: In recent announcements, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 6.2%, marking an increase of 0.5% from the previous month. The number of jobless individuals now stands at 9.3 million, a significant rise of 700,000 people seeking employment. These findings highlight the ongoing economic challenges faced by the nation and the need for further support and intervention to address the growing unemployment issue.

data.title: Top Wall Street Forecasters Revamp Constellation Brands Expectations Ahead Of Q4 Earnings
data.description: Constellation Brands, Inc. (NYSE: STZ) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings on April 8, with analysts predicting earnings of $1.71 per share, a decrease from $2.63 per share a year ago. The expected quarterly revenue is $1.88 billion, down from $2.16 billion last year. Nicholas Fink was recently appointed as the next president and CEO of Constellation Brands. The company's shares rose by 2.7% to $155.25 on Monday. For more information and detailed analyst ratings, readers can visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Tryg - Transactions in connection with share buyback programme
data.description: Tryg A/S declared on 22 January 2026 that its Board of Directors approved a share repurchase program of a maximum DKK 1.0 billion. The endeavor complies with the EU Market Abuse Regulation, EU Regulation no. 596/2014, and Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052. The share buyback scheme is set to close by 13 May 2026 at the latest.
